name: Fried Vegetarian Spring Rolls (Cha Gio Chay)
recipeIngredient: Thin rice vermicelli (bun), 8 ounces OR Japanese alimentary paste noodles (somen)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all filling ingredients in a large mixing bowl and blend well with hands. Set aside.
2. Dissolve sugar in warm water to create sweetened water for the rice paper.
3. Work with 4 sheets of rice paper at a time, keeping remaining sheets covered with a damp cloth.
4. Immerse each rice paper sheet in sweetened water briefly, then lift and lay flat on a towel.
5. Fold the bottom third of each round, place 1 teaspoon of filling in the center, press into a compact rectangle.
6. Fold sides over the mixture, then roll from bottom to top to enclose the filling completely.
7. Continue until all filling is used (rolls can be prepared up to 1 day in advance; refrigerate).
8. Fry rolls: Heat oil in skillets to 325F. Add rolls in batches without touching, frying for 10-12 minutes, turning often, until golden and crisp.
9. Remove with tongs, drain on paper towels, and keep warm in a low oven until all rolls are cooked.
10. To serve, wrap each roll in lettuce leaf with noodles and selected vegetable platter items, dipping into sauce.
Yield: 40 rolls.
(Note: Fried rolls can be frozen, thawed, and reheated in a 350F oven to crisp and heat through.)
